title: Austrodanthonia geniculata
taxonomy: Austrodanthonia geniculata is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta and the class Liliopsida. It belongs to the family Poaceae and the subfamily Danthonioideae. Further, it is placed within the genus Austrodanthonia, which is a group of grasses native to Australia. The species name geniculata refers to the curved or bent stems of the plant. Within the Poaceae family, Austrodanthonia geniculata is closely related to other species of bent grasses and wallaby grasses.
uses: Austrodanthonia geniculata, also known as wallaby grass, is native to eastern Australia. Although limited information is available about its medicinal uses, some studies suggest that the plant has potential anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used in traditional medicine by Indigenous Australians to treat various ailments. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Austrodanthonia geniculata for medicinal purposes. Further studies are required to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits.

It is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes.
